summary:
Although no direct documentation confirms it, SATB1’s structural role in organizing chromatin loops at matrix attachment regions—often composed of AT-rich DNA—and its functional similarity to ATP-dependent remodelers targeting AT-rich sites provides strong indirect evidence linking it to AT-rich sequences. Therefore, based on molecular mechanism rather than empirical data from retrievals alone, SATB1 is reasonably considered to position itself near such sequences.
